stem: A parallelogram has one angle of $115°$. Find the size of the opposite angle.
hint: Opposite angles in a parallelogram are equal.
explanation: Opposite angles in a parallelogram are equal, so the opposite angle is also $115°$.

stem: A parallelogram has one angle of $72°$. Find the size of the adjacent angle.
hint: Adjacent angles in a parallelogram are supplementary — they sum to $180°$.
explanation: Adjacent angles in a parallelogram are co-interior angles and sum to $180°$. So the adjacent angle is $180° - 72° = 108°$.

stem: A rectangle has one angle labeled $(3x + 6)°$. Find the value of $x$.
hint: All interior angles of a rectangle are $90°$.
explanation: Since all angles in a rectangle are $90°$, we have $3x + 6 = 90$. Solving: $3x = 84$, so $x = 28$.

stem: Three angles of a quadrilateral measure $80°$, $100°$, and $95°$. Find the size of the fourth angle.
hint: The interior angles of any quadrilateral sum to $360°$.
explanation: The angle sum of a quadrilateral is $360°$. So the fourth angle is $360° - 80° - 100° - 95° = 85°$.

stem: A rhombus has one angle of $50°$. Find the size of the adjacent angle.
hint: In a rhombus, adjacent angles are supplementary.
explanation: In a rhombus, adjacent angles sum to $180°$ (they are co-interior angles between parallel sides). So the adjacent angle is $180° - 50° = 130°$.

stem: A trapezium has one pair of parallel sides. One angle between the parallel sides on the same side of a non-parallel leg measures $73°$. Find the size of the co-interior angle on the same side.
hint: Co-interior angles between parallel lines sum to $180°$.
explanation: Co-interior angles between parallel lines sum to $180°$. So the co-interior angle is $180° - 73° = 107°$.

stem: Kite $ABCD$ has $\angle A = 100°$ and $\angle C = 40°$. Angles $\angle B$ and $\angle D$ are equal. Find the size of $\angle B$.
hint: The angle sum of a quadrilateral is $360°$. In a kite, one pair of opposite angles is equal.
explanation: $\angle B + \angle D = 360° - 100° - 40° = 220°$. Since $\angle B = \angle D$, each is $220° \div 2 = 110°$.

stem: An isosceles trapezium has base angles of $65°$ each. Find the size of each of the two remaining angles.
hint: Co-interior angles between the parallel sides sum to $180°$.
explanation: Each base angle and its co-interior angle sum to $180°$. So each remaining angle is $180° - 65° = 115°$.

stem: Parallelogram $ABCD$ has $\angle A = (3x + 15)°$ and $\angle B = (2x + 10)°$. Find the value of $x$.
hint: Adjacent angles in a parallelogram are supplementary.
explanation: Adjacent angles sum to $180°$: $(3x + 15) + (2x + 10) = 180$. Simplifying: $5x + 25 = 180$, so $5x = 155$ and $x = 31$.

stem: In quadrilateral $ABCD$, $\angle A = 90°$, $\angle B = (x + 20)°$, $\angle C = 2x°$, and $\angle D = (x - 10)°$. Find the value of $x$.
hint: The angle sum of a quadrilateral is $360°$.

stem: The diagonals of a rhombus bisect its angles. One diagonal bisects an angle of the rhombus, creating two half-angles of $36°$ each. Find the size of the adjacent angle of the rhombus.
hint: First find the full angle that was bisected, then use the fact that adjacent angles in a rhombus sum to $180°$.
explanation: The bisected angle is $2 \times 36° = 72°$. Since adjacent angles in a rhombus sum to $180°$, the adjacent angle is $180° - 72° = 108°$.

stem: A diagonal of a parallelogram creates two alternate interior angles. The angles are $(4x - 20)°$ and $(2x + 30)°$. Find the value of $x$.
explanation: Alternate interior angles are equal: $4x - 20 = 2x + 30$. Solving: $2x = 50$, so $x = 25$.
